fields.default: On the amount PAID to the vendor within a slab window; the payment date picks the slab %, capped to net sale volume of this manufacturer's products. Vendor issues a credit note (slab% x paid) -> log as a Debit Note to the supplier.
fields.label: CD Basis

This one page controls the whole StockPilot suite — every report, number card and button reads these values live. Changing a cutoff or frequency reclassifies items and reshuffles count due-dates (the schedule anchor keeps dates stable for a given configuration).

fields.description: Fast Moving: items inside the top X% of cumulative 12-month consumption value are class A.
fields.label: Movement A Cutoff (%)

fields.description: Medium Movement: between the A cutoff and this. Everything beyond is C (Slow Moving).
fields.label: Movement B Cutoff (%)
Value Classes (A/B/C by current stock value)

fields.description: High Value: items inside the top X% of cumulative current stock value are class A.
fields.label: Value A Cutoff (%)

fields.description: Medium Value: between the A cutoff and this. Everything beyond is C (Low Value).
fields.label: Value B Cutoff (%)

fields.description: X = steady all year: the top-3 months hold LESS than this share of 24-month demand value.
fields.label: X (Steady) Below (%)

fields.description: Y = seasonal window: top-3-month share up to this. Above it the item is Z (sharp spike).
fields.label: Y (Seasonal) Up To (%)

fields.description: Rotation start date for the Count Schedule stagger. The report's own Schedule Anchor filter overrides this per run. Changing it re-staggers every item's count dates.
fields.label: Schedule Anchor Date

fields.description: Negative-stock items are counted at the Negative Lane frequency (below) regardless of class - your worst book errors get counted first. An item leaves this lane as soon as a count fixes its balance.
fields.label: Priority-count negative stock

fields.description: How often each negative-stock item comes up. Daily workload = negatives / this. e.g. 126 negatives at 7 d = 18 a day; at 14 d = 9 a day. Independent of the AA frequency.
fields.label: Negative Lane Frequency (days)

fields.description: A missed item stays on the Day Schedule for this many days until it is counted. Applies once an item has been counted at least once. 0 disables catch-up.
fields.label: Catch-up Grace (days)

fields.description: Count due-dates use a frozen class snapshot so rolling sales can't reshuffle the calendar. Refreshes automatically (below) or via the report's Refresh Class Freeze button. Analytical reports stay live.
fields.label: Freeze classes for scheduling

fields.description: A daily scheduler re-freezes the classes once this many months have passed since the last freeze.
fields.label: Auto-refresh Freeze Every (months)

fields.description: When the scheduling classes were last frozen.
fields.label: Classes Frozen On

fields.description: Days a supplier typically takes to deliver. Reorder point = daily demand x lead time + safety stock.
fields.label: Lead Time (days)

fields.description: Target availability. Higher = bigger safety stock (z factor 1.28 / 1.65 / 1.96 / 2.33).
fields.label: Service Level (%)

fields.description: Suggested order quantity covers lead time + this many days of demand.
fields.label: Order Cover (days)

fields.description: An item with stock on hand but no sales for this many months counts as dead. The report's Idle Months filter overrides this per run.
fields.label: Dead After (months)
